meaning: I have received your message and will comply with the instruction.
operationalMeaning: Wilco means compliance, not just acknowledgement — use when you will follow the instruction.
whoUsesIt: Pilots to ATC; occasionally military or specific procedures.
whenUsed: When an instruction requires action and a simple readback is not required or after a full readback.
whyUsed: Confirms the crew will execute — stronger than roger alone.

realExamples.text: ANAC 123, if unable RVSM, descend flight level two eight zero.
realExamples.speaker: Center

realExamples.text: Wilco, descending flight level two eight zero, ANAC 123.
realExamples.speaker: Pilot

pilotResponses: Wilco, descending flight level two eight zero, ANAC 123.
icaoQuestions: When is wilco appropriate instead of a full readback?
What is the difference between wilco and roger?
commonMistakes: Saying wilco when you only received information — use roger.
Using wilco without intending to comply immediately.
Overusing wilco on complex clearances that need a full readback.
pronunciationTips: Do not say 'will comply' and 'wilco' in the same transmission.

compareTerms.note: Roger means received; wilco means received and will comply.
Affirm means yes to a question; wilco is compliance with an instruction.

didYouKnow: Wilco is shortened from 'will comply' — use sparingly in civil IFR.
captainStory: Busy sector over Brasília, Center asked us to expedite descent for traffic — 'Wilco' after the readback kept the frequency short.
